name: Get Enabled GTTs
summary: Retrieves the list of GTT (Good Till Triggered) alert types enabled for the logged-in user. A POST call carrying the user's uid returns an array of supported alert types (ai_ts), each element exposing an ai_t code such as ATP or LTP.

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

json_fields.name: uid*
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: GTT order success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: ai_ts
response_fields.description: Array of alert types. Each element is an object with an ai_t field (e.g. {"ai_t":"ATP"}, {"ai_t":"LTP"}).

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: Error message present only in case of failure (e.g. Session Expired : Invalid Session Key). Sourced from the sample failure response; not listed in the RESPONSE DETAILS table.

notes: Despite the GET-sounding name, this is a POST request. Parameters are sent as URL-encoded form data: jData (a JSON object) and jKey, joined with '&' (jData={...}&jKey=...), not as a pure JSON body. The curl example shows 'https://BaseURL/GetEnabledGTTs' as a placeholder; the real base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI. request_time in the success sample is a timestamp string in DDMMYYYYHHMMSS format (e.g. 04062021121503). ai_ts is an array of objects each carrying a single 'ai_t' alert-type code (e.g. ATP, LTP). jKey is the session token obtained at login. The RESPONSE DETAILS table on page 35 lists only stat, request_time and ai_ts with no possible-value cells; stat's Ok/Not_Ok values are inferred from the samples; emsg is added from the failure sample, not the table.
issues: None substantive. The section is fully contained on page 35 and ends where PLACE OCO ORDER begins on page 36. The request param table lists jData*/jKey* (no possible-value cells); the jData JSON-fields table lists only uid* (no possible-value cell); the response table lists stat/request_time/ai_ts (no possible-value cells). stat's possible values (Ok / Not_Ok) are inferred from the success/failure samples, not an explicit cell. emsg is documented from the sample failure response, not the response table. curl and both JSON samples are verbatim.
